senses.gloss: passive participle of أَسْنَدَ (ʔasnada).; backed-up, fortified, held-up (describes having an unverifiable chain of narrators for a Prophetic Hadith alleging its authenticity)
musnad (collection of Prophetic Hadiths with narrators)

examples.english: The Musnad of Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal

etymology.text:
Derived from the passive participle of أَسْنَدَ (ʔasnada, “to support”).

Tool noun from the root س ن د (s n d).
